literal: To get wet to a dry thread.
explanation_en: To be completely soaked, dripping wet.
To live under a rock, to have born yesterday.
To be in a bad mood.

literal: A matter not suffering a corpse.
explanation_en: A matter requiring immediate action. Polish word for "corpse" is "zwłoki", and it's "zwłoka" is a homonim for "delay".
Tights worn on the legs. "Raj" means "paradise, "stopy" means "feet".
The retina of the eye. Polish word "siatkówka" means either "volleyball" or "retina".
It symbolizes a remedy for jealousy, envy, frustration and hate.
A desirable outcome justifies questionable actions. "Środki" is plural for middle of sth, may also be "remedy" or "means".

literal: Undergo like a dog to a hedgehog.
explanation_en: To approach something with reluctance and caution.
No offense. Polish word "bez" is a homonym for "elderberry" or "without", "obrazy" are "pictures", "obraza" is "offense".

literal: In the face of a hedgehog.

explanation_en: Freedom of speech. Homonim "wolny" may either mean "freed" or "slow".

literal: A wolf carried away a few times and was carried away itself.
original: Nosił wilk razy kilka, ponieśli i wilka.
explanation_en: One can get away with bad behaviour only a few times before it is noticed and punished.
